description: Upstream BMS (/SESOWABMSPD04) polled the wrong register for ~87 days. Raw hourly samples alternate between the real counter (~7k MWh band), a near-zero artifact (< 100), and a stuck-high value (~805k). Currently handled by 4-segment split (:d.A | :d.B | :d.C | :d.D) that flat-carries across the gaps, losing ~484 MWh of real consumption across the window. A bracket + interpolate upgrade was attempted and reverted; see reference/docs/snv_anga_followups.md.
Counter froze 2026-02-19 at value 12121.072. Ontology stitches raw segment (:d.raw, valid_to 2026-02-19) with a children-sum patch (:d.patch, sum of B217.Å1_VMM71:d.D) via rolling_sum.

Children-sum patch: recovers B216 consumption from its sole child B217.Å1_VMM71 after B216's counter froze. Assumes B217 captures all B216 downstream flow; the ~28 MWh Feb-2026 vs-Excel delta is the pre-freeze (Feb 1 → Feb 18) portion that Excel's STRUX register-diff absorbed as zero.
Counter frozen at 12737.1533 for the entire observed window; last real reading predates the 2025-01-01 ingest start. Patched from child B327.Å1_VMM71 via sum from 2026-02-07 onward.

Children-sum patch: B327.Å1_VMM71 (sole child) stands in for B308's frozen counter. Produces meter_net ≈ 0 (as children equal parent). Feb-2026 Excel shows −30.78 vs ontology −8.98 MWh; the ~22 MWh gap is pre-patch Feb-1 → Feb-7 that the ontology does not reconstruct.

B307.Å1_VMM71 trunk counter frozen at 2227.06 MWh from 2025-01-01 through October 2025 (only +0.57 MWh drift over 10 months — effectively dead). Counter unfreezes and reads normally from 2025-11-01 onward. B307 is a pool meter whose children (B216, B302, B304, B311, B334, B337, B339) report real consumption during the freeze, so meter_net shows strong negative values Jan–Oct 2025 (~ −1500 to −2600 MWh/month, cumulative −22 GWh). Not patched: pre-November trunk consumption is unknown, and both zero-patching and backward interpolation would introduce artifacts. The split at 2025-07-31 straddles a non-event (register was already frozen pre-swap and stayed flat post-swap), kept in place since rolling_sum stitching is harmless on flat values.

Post-unfreeze ontology totals lag Excel by ~3 months. Ontology ticks Nov=7.5, Dec=9.7, Jan=2.0, Feb=9.3 GWh. Excel shows zero until Feb 2026, then Feb=7.8, Mar=10.9, Apr=0.26 GWh — same cumulative ~27 GWh but shifted ~3 months later. Source of the offset unknown: delayed Excel invoicing, wrong Snowflake timestamps, or a genuine mid-window meter commissioning that accounting dated differently from BMS. Needs site verification before any corrective action.
Device register swap on 2025-07-31. Handled by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B stitched via rolling_sum.
Device swap on 2026-01-13 (last value 3019.23). Handled by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B stitched via rolling_sum.

Counter went offline 2025-07-31 at 328545. Downstream of B325 Panna2; no children to patch from — consumption beyond this date is unrecoverable from B390 alone. Leaf meter relative to its intended topology branch.
Counter went offline 2025-07-25 at 133.0. Leaf meter; no children available for a sum-patch.
Counter went offline 2025-11-26 at near-zero value (0.00213...). Leaf meter; no children available for a sum-patch.

Excel row 23 (B307 pool formula) subtracts B330.Å1_VMM71, and row 46 (B337) subtracts the same meter — B330 is double-counted in the Excel totals. Ontology attaches B330 to B337 per decisions.md 2026-04-20; B307 therefore over-counts in topology by Δ(B330) each month (~0.9 MWh Jan-2026, ~27 MWh Feb-2026). Excel is immutable; this discrepancy is expected and permanent.

Direction conflict between flow_schema and naming extractor for the B311.Å1_VMM70 ↔ B311.Å1_VMM71 edge. Not yet resolved in decisions.md. Does not affect Jan/Feb 2026 totals because both meters are leaf-level relative to their building's accounting.

Bracket patch: parameter-free monotone value-range clip over :d.raw's readings. Endpoints (v_lo, v_hi) derive from the source's readings immediately outside the validity window — the last clean pre-corruption value and the first clean post-corruption value. Any reading whose value falls outside [v_lo, v_hi] is physically impossible for a monotone counter and is dropped.

Counter offline 2025-09-21 through 2026-01-13 (~116 days), full catchup flush on 2026-01-14 (+135.62 kWh). Total preserved: 135.75 kWh ÷ 116 days = 1.17 kWh/day, matching neighbor rates (~1.2/day). Patched via interpolate between clean endpoints 2025-09-20 (483.323) and 2026-01-14 (619.070).

Partial data loss: counter flat 2025-07-14 through 2025-07-23 (~10 days), resumed with only +2.7 kWh caught up across Jul 24-25. Pre/post neighbor rate ~1.85/day suggests ~22 kWh expected; ~19 kWh was lost (counter stopped counting, not just reporting). Not patched via interpolate — that would under-distribute. Magnitude is small (~0.05% of annual) so accepted as-is.
Three-event cluster inside the corruption window: glitch 2025-08-01 to 2025-08-03, offline 2025-08-06, register swap 2025-08-07 at value 803864.576. Segment split produced :d.A | :d.B | :d.C.
End-of-corruption glitch boundary where the :d.C → :d.D segment split was applied (last stuck-high value observed: 805175.296). Counter resumes cleanly on 2025-09-28.
Device register swap on 2025-07-31 (last value 2227.0615). Handled by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B stitched via rolling_sum.

B315's meter also covers B357's steam consumption. Per the reference formula doc ("Mäts tillsammans med B357"), B357 has no standalone ÅNGA meter; its usage is bundled into B315's reading. Affects per-building attribution if B357 is queried alone — the value reported for B315 includes B357.

ÅNGA supply to B312 is shut off; any residual consumption is metered together with B385 per the reference formula doc ("Ånga är avstängd till byggnad så mäts tillsammans med B385"). B312 has no standalone ÅNGA meter, so per-building attribution will blend B312 into B385.

B330 accounting in PME covers both B330 and B331 as one combined unit per the reference formula doc ("B330/331 är det som gäller i PME"). B331 has no standalone ÅNGA formula; its consumption is embedded in B330's three-meter sum (VMM71 + VMM72 + VMM73).

B307 is fed from two directions. Per the reference formula doc ("Matning från 2 håll, utgår att media tar kortaste vägen") accounting assumes steam takes the shortest path to each downstream consumer. The 10-term building formula subtracts B216, B339, B302.VMM73, B311, B334, B304, B330, B337 from (B307.VMM71 + B307.VMM72). Relevant when interpreting pool residuals on B307.

B308 is fed from two directions; the current building formula (B308.VMM71 − B327.VMM71) is under review by site engineer Anton per the reference formula doc ("Anton kollar upp formeln"). Two meters at B308 are scheduled for replacement at Sommarstopp 2025 — expect formula revision after the swap.

Only the carbon filter at B339 currently consumes ÅNGA per the reference formula doc ("Endast kolfiltret som använder ånga"). Post-Sommarstopp 2025 B339 will additionally feed the 200-series buildings (B201–B209, currently all "avstängd" / N/A), enabling future line-loss calculations for the 339 → 200-houses spur.

B325 is being rebuilt and will be producer-only post-rebuild (no ÅNGA consumption). Current formula B325 = Panna2 + Panna3 − B390 is flagged "Osäkert värde" (uncertain value) in the reference formula doc. Post- Sommarstopp 2025 the trunk-export accounting will change: B325 will take over metering of the full B390 trunk. Revise building formula after the rebuild lands.

Per the reference formula doc's Mätarlista: B325.Panna2_MWH is the electric boiler (Elpanna). B325.Panna3_MWH is the oil boiler burning HVO biodiesel (Oljepanna HVO-olja). Both are producers feeding the B390 trunk, not consumers.

Reference formula doc lists planned ÅNGA meter changes for the Sommarstopp 2025 window: (1) B325 to take over full B390 trunk metering; (2) two B308 meters to be replaced (formula under Anton's review); (3) B339 to redirect and feed the 200-series buildings (enabling 339→200-houses loss calculations); (4) B302: one meter to be reset to zero; (5) B616 (GTN) swap — already executed 2025-11-05, see GTN B616_swap. Several SNV building formulas may need revision post-Sommarstopp.

Several ÅNGA meters use transitional "VM7x" names in Snowflake (instead of the stable "VMM7x") per the reference formula doc ("heter VM71 än så länge. Kommer döpas om på sikt till VMM71"). Affected SNV meters: B216.Å1_VM71, B217.Å1_VM71, B313.Å1_VM71. Ontology meter_ids already use the canonical VMM71 form; the Snowflake crosswalk currently maps VMM71 → VM71 for these. When the upstream rename lands, the crosswalk must prefer the new VMM7x variant (consistent with the "_E" variant preference rule).

Sibling effect of B317.Å1_VMM72 patch: the catch-up redistribution in B317 moves consumption out of Jan 2026 and into the offline window; B310 ANGA roll-up absorbs that redistribution as a small negative residual vs Excel. Accepted side-effect of the interpolate patch chosen for B317.

Oil boiler (Oljepanna HVO) counter flat from 2025-07-12 onward, but Excel cache shows 3419 MWh Jan / 3391 MWh Feb 2026 on building B325. Implies either the boiler is idle (Excel value is stale / manually entered) or the counter is dead while the boiler still runs. Needs site verification before the Jan/Feb comparison for B325 can be trusted.
Auxiliary sub-meter for B216 stays flat at zero across the full reference period (2025-01 → 2026-02). B216's actual steam consumption flows through B216.Å1_VM71. Expected inactivity.

Auxiliary meters B302.Å1_VMM72 and B302.Å1_VMM73 stay flat at zero for the full reference window. The building's steam consumption flows entirely through B302.Å1_VMM71 with Excel cache showing ~194/247 MWh Jan/Feb. Expected inactivity.
B305.Å1_VMM71 flat across the whole reference period (2025-01 → 2026-02). Excel cache confirms B305 ÅNGA = 0 both months — the building has no steam consumption in this window. Expected.

B313.Å1_VMM72 flat at zero across the reference period. B313's consumption is entirely through B313.Å1_VM71 (which also has a long flat run, see ann-snv-anga-b313-offline). Excel cache shows B313 ÅNGA = 0. Expected inactivity.
B341.Å1_VMM71 flat at zero for the whole reference period. No Excel ÅNGA row for B341. Building either has no steam connection active or the meter is dormant. Expected inactivity.
B330.Å1_VMM73 mostly flat with tiny increments (max ~0.08 MWh/day). B330 consumption is dominated by B330.Å1_VMM71 and B330.Å1_VMM72; VMM73 contributes negligibly (< 1% of building total).

Several SNV ÅNGA meters show multi-week flat runs in summer (June–August) — normal seasonal dormancy because steam demand collapses when process loads shut down. Examples: B301 (~53 days flat), B304 (~32 days flat), B325.Panna2/Panna3 (summer shutdowns). Not a data quality issue; expected behaviour.
Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9999063 on 2025-12-03 (Δ=-9996275). Readings resume normally. Stitched via rolling_sum of :d.A + :d.B segments.
Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9998599 on 2025-09-15 (Δ=-9994911). Stitched via rolling_sum.
Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9998597 on 2025-06-08. Stitched via rolling_sum.

Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9998733 on 2026-01-04 (inside comparison window). Stitched via rolling_sum. B330 Jan shows -3728 kWh drift vs Excel — likely a stitch-boundary timing offset around the swap date.

Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9995275 on 2026-01-29 (inside comparison window, 3 days from month-end). Stitched via rolling_sum. B337 Jan shows -11023 kWh drift vs Excel — stitch-boundary offset.
Counter rollover at Schneider ceiling (~10,000,000). Counter reset from 9996659 on 2025-05-21. Stitched via rolling_sum. Swap well before comparison window.

Meter offline 2025-02-28 (frozen at 56655.14). Leaf meter — no children to patch from. B203 +term. Flow absent from ontology from Feb 2025; B203 still matches within 1 kWh in 2026-01/02, suggesting this meter's historical contribution was small or not in the flow window.
Meter offline 2026-02-19 (frozen at 0.39). Low absolute value. Used as B307 -term sub-feeder.
Meter offline 2026-02-22 (frozen at 0.284). Low value.
Meter offline 2026-02-22 (frozen at 0.295). Low value.
Meter offline 2025-12-10 (frozen at 607512). Referenced by B310 (-0.5×), B311 (-0.5×), B317 (+1.0×). Pre-outage data captured; post-outage contribution zero in both ontology and Excel.
Meter offline 2026-01-19 (frozen at 0.209). Low absolute value so impact negligible.

Ställverk summary meter referenced by B310 (+0.5×) and B311 (+0.5×). NOT in Snowflake BMS — only in STRUX (verified: 79168 kWh Jan 2026, 77654 Feb 2026). Per RESOLVE_ONTOLOGY §0 no STRUX synthesis. Topology feeds 0 to B310/B311 via this pool; residual ~6 MWh/mo per building represents the T26S trunk-vs-subs gap. Documented gap; B310/B311 still match within ±3%.

B334.T87, T88, T89, T91, T92 are STRUX-only summary meters. Excel references them as +terms for B334. Snowflake carries per-feeder sub-meters (underscore notation). Topology fix (2026-04-20): derived timeseries with aggregation=sum from BMS sub-feeders. Brings B334 from -100% to +7% — small residual (+30000 kWh/mo) is genuine STRUX-vs-BMS fidelity gap, not a topology bug.

B209.T21 and B209.T32 are STRUX-only trunks. Excel B209 = T21 - T32 - sub-meters. T21 is +term but has no Snowflake data; B209 topology reads 0 vs Excel 15244 Jan. Could be fixed in the future by creating derived sums from BMS sub-meters (T21-1, T21-2-1, T21-4-1, etc.); not applied in v1 because the BMS sub-feeders don't fully cover the same electrical domain as T21 (some unmetered taps in between).

B304's two +term meters (B313.T26S-3-12 at 4477 kWh Jan, B336.T40-3-1 at 0 kWh) are both STRUX-only with no BMS equivalents. Topology can't populate B304 without synthesizing STRUX values (forbidden). B304 reads 0 in ontology vs 4477 Jan Excel.

Resolved 2026-04-22: reference formula doc specifies B385 = +B339.T77-4-5 +B312.T34-2-5. Previously-parsed Excel had +B307.T77-4-5-B (phantom meter, not in Snowflake). meter_allocations now uses the real B339.T77-4-5; B385 diff ≤ 1 kWh both months.
Excel row 50 W column = literal string 'Reservkraft pl7' — placeholder for backup-power allocation. Excluded from facit_meters. B341 matches within ±0.002%, so placeholder evaluates to zero in STRUX.
Excel R28=0.85 applies to whole (T34 - T34-2-3 - T34-2-4 - T34-2-5) net. Fix: B312.EL_VIRT virtual fed from B312.T34 at 0.85, residual 0.15 to SNV.EL_UNALLOC campus sink. B312 matches exactly.
B209.T32-4-2 split 75%/25% B204/B205. Implemented via feeds to B204.EL_VIRT @ 0.75 and B205.EL_VIRT @ 0.25. Both buildings match exactly.

T49 pool split: B310 @ 0.4, B313 @ 0.1, B317 @ 0.5. Fix (2026-04-20): feeds edges + 8 extra-sub compensation edges (subs subtracted from B310's formula at 0.4 but not from B313/B317's formulas are fed separately to B313@0.1 and B317@0.5). B310 matches within 3%, B313 matches within 1 kWh, B317 Jan matches within 5 kWh. Feb B317 has -56% diff attributable to anomalous Excel Feb cached value (2× Jan despite similar T49 flow) — likely Excel data-entry issue.
T26S pool split: B310 @ 0.5, B311 @ 0.5. T26S is STRUX-only so the pool residual (T26S - subs) is not computable; feeds contribute 0 through this path. Residual ~6 MWh/mo per building. Annotated.
B311.T29 split: B310 @ 1/3, B311 @ 2/3. Fix: feeds edges. Both buildings' contributions from T29 now match Excel exactly.

B339.T77-4-5 is +1.0 in both B305 (row 22 helper) and B392 (row 73) — Excel bookkeeping counts the same meter twice. Compromise: 50/50 feeds split to B305.EL_VIRT and B392.EL_VIRT. Each building's Excel expects 100% of T77-4-5 (46018 kWh Jan); topology gives 50% to each. Residual: B305 -21%, B392 -42%. No way to satisfy Excel's 2.0 total without synthesizing data.
B318.T21-6-2-A is +1.0 in both B318 (row 34) and B344 (row 53). Same double-+1.0 accounting pattern as T77-4-5. 50/50 compromise; residual -1.9% B318, -4.3% B344.
Complex formula row 23 (B307): trunk summaries T10-1, T11-1 + 14 sub-feeders. Added hasSubMeter edges trunk→sub (14 edges). Building matches within 0-68 kWh.
Complex formula row 48 (B339): trunk summaries T71-1..T78-1 + 4 sub-feeders. Added hasSubMeter edges trunk→sub (4 edges). Building matches within 0-106 kWh.

Complex formula row 22 (B305): sum of 11 +terms across multiple buildings' prefixes. All at coefficient 1.0. Attribution by lowest-row rule; matches within ±6 kWh before T77-4-5 50/50 split. After split: -21%/-22% from the T77-4-5 half-share.

B317 Feb 2026 Excel cached value (134118 kWh) is 2× Jan (62941 kWh) despite similar T49 trunk flow in both months. Ontology gives 58643 Feb, consistent with T49 Feb flow and matching Jan's scale. Likely Excel formula evaluation error or month-specific STRUX override. Jan now matches (diff ≈ 5 kWh after removing the duplicate −B317.T49-5-9 term) but Feb still reads −75k drift. Flagged for upstream Excel audit.

B334.T87, T88, T89, T91, T92 lack Snowflake readings. Applied a derived timeseries with aggregation=sum sourcing from their Snowflake sub-feeders (underscore notation: T87_1_1, T87_2_2, etc.). Bridged B334 from -100% to +7.2% — remaining delta is the natural STRUX-vs-BMS measurement fidelity gap. 20 BMS sub-meter IDs added to facit_meters at campus attribution.

SNV EL monthly reconciliation rows marked 'match' may show non-zero diffs up to ~10 kWh (relative < 0.005%). These are floating-point accumulation artifacts from 100+ sub-meter sums and are not real discrepancies. The comparison classifier uses a 0.1% relative threshold for 'match'.
Drift of ~2 MWh (0.1%) between topology and Excel, both Jan and Feb 2026. Root cause not yet classified. Low magnitude; flagged for follow-up but not blocking reconciliation.
Monthly reconciliation residual below 0.05% of total — floating-point aggregation noise across many EL meters. No documented meter swap; accepted as pipeline precision limit.
Monthly reconciliation residual below 0.05% of total — floating-point aggregation noise across B326 meters. Accepted as pipeline precision limit.

−8.6% drift vs Excel both months. Both + terms (T57-4-7, T21-6-2-A) also appear as hasSubMeter children of their naming parents, likely double-counted under the one-parent-per-meter rule. Open; see open_questions.md §6.

Complex-formula undercount (−23/−21 MWh both months) — same magnitude as B305, likely same root cause (helper-row sub-meters zeroed by hasSubMeter edges that attached them to their naming parents). Open; see open_questions.md.

SNV EL building formulas cross-checked against reference/reference_formula_doc/the_only_one_formula_doc.xlsx (EL sheet). Surgical fixes applied: B385 +B307.T77-4-5-B → +B339.T77-4-5 (resolved 23k+ MWh phantom issue); B392 +B339.T77-4-5 → +B334.T87-5-2; B317 duplicate −B317.T49-5-9 removed (Jan now matches); B318.T21-6-2-A sign flipped (+ → −). New buildings added as documentation-only (B324, B399, B209 UPS, B325 Luft; their meters are attributed at campus level since Excel cache has 0/no row for these — avoids introducing phantom drift). B339 KOLF allocation added for doc-symmetry (B339 Kolfilter runtime unchanged). STRUX-only buildings (B340, B371, B336) cannot be populated from Snowflake — annotated.

Sub-aggregator listed in reference formula doc as B209 UPS = +B339.T77-5-1. Excel cache has no B209 UPS row. B339.T77-5-1 exists in Snowflake and is attributed to B339 (where it is subtracted, matching Excel). The B209 UPS aggregator stays as a documentation-only allocation row; its readings aren't double-counted against any building.

Sub-aggregator per reference formula doc: B325 Luft = +B325.T13-5-6 +B325.T13-6-3 +B325.T12-3-4 +B325.T12-5-3 +B325.T12-6-3. Excel cache has no B325 Luft row. Of the 5 terms, 3 exist in Snowflake (T12-3-4, T13-5-6, T13-6-3) added as campus-level meters; 2 are missing (T12-5-3, T12-6-3 — suspected doc T12/T13 typo, see snv_el_b325_luft_missing_terms). Aggregator is documentation-only (allocation-side) to avoid double-counting against B325.

Reference formula doc references B325.T12-5-3 and B325.T12-6-3 which are absent from Snowflake. Snowflake has B325.T13-5-3 and B325.T13-6-3 — suspected doc typo (T12 → T13). Needs site confirmation. Impact: B325 Luft aggregator (documentation-only) covers 3 of 5 doc-listed meters.

The B339 carbon-filter sub-load has always existed in the ontology as building 'B339 Kolfilter' with meter B339.T74-2-2 (Excel cache ~16.8 / 17.2 MWh Jan/Feb). Reference formula doc calls this same aggregator 'B339 KOLF'. A parallel 'B339 KOLF' allocation row was added for doc-symmetry but does not alter runtime numbers (meter_measures already attributes T74-2-2 to B339 Kolfilter). The T74-2-2 term was also removed from B339's main formula (where the parser had incorrectly included it).

B324 EL intake now modeled via B324.EL_INTAKE virtual: B324.H3 + B324.H4_1 feeds k=1.0 with hasSubMeter fan-out to all 92 root downstream EL meters. Sums to AZ-total EL within 0.01 % of TN-side billing per Total SNV tab. See ann-snv-el-b324-intake-virtual + ann-snv-el-b324-h4-1-rollovers.

Reference formula doc lists B399 = +B328.T19-3-2 +B328.T20-3-2 but Excel cache has no B399 row. Meters added to ontology as campus-level (building_id blank) so the raw data is ingestable but not attributed to any building (avoids creating a phantom drift vs Excel=0). Excel's omission of B399 is the source of truth for v1.
Added missing +B311.T80-2-3 term to B303's allocation per reference formula doc; meter exists in Snowflake. Previous ontology had only 2 of 3 doc-listed terms.

Reference formula doc defines B340 = +B371.T23_2_6. All B371.* sub-meters are STRUX-only (absent from Snowflake BMS), so the ontology cannot populate B340 without a STRUX-synthesis primitive. Per project convention (STRUX-only never synthesized), B340 reads zero in topology while Excel cached value will show real consumption.

Reference formula doc defines B371 = +B371.T23 + B371.T31 − B371.T31_4_4 − B371.T31_5 − B371.T31_4_3 − B371.T23_4_4 (6 terms). All B371.* meters are STRUX-only (absent from Snowflake). Ontology cannot populate B371 without STRUX synthesis; reads zero in topology.

Reference formula doc defines B336 EL = +B336.T39 + B336.T40 − B336.T40_3_1 despite B336 being marked demolished ('Utgår byggnads rivs') for VÄRME and KALLVATTEN. All B336.* sub-meters are STRUX-only (absent from Snowflake). Status unclear: either electric systems survive demolition (decommissioning loads) or the doc is not yet in sync with the demolition. Needs site confirmation.

Reference formula doc adds +B360.T99_7_4_A +B360.T99_7_5 to B310's massive pool formula. Both meters exist in Snowflake. Not currently added because B360 is not a registered building in the ontology (would require building + sub-meter infrastructure). Impact: B310 topology slightly under-counts vs Excel by the sum of these two sub-feeders. Flagged for follow-up — add B360 building to ontology with its T99 sub-meters, or mirror the two meters as virtual B310 sub-feeds.

Reference formula doc references B313.T26S_2_16 and B313.T26S_2_24 in the B310 (−0.5×) and B311 (−0.5×, +0.5× respectively) pool terms. Neither exists in Snowflake. Current ontology uses B313.T26S-2-6 and B313.T26S-2-XX instead — B313.T26S-2-XX is a real Snowflake meter, B313.T26S-2-6 is not. The Snowflake fleet has B313.T26S-2-12 but not -2-16 or -2-24. Suspected naming/parsing mismatch between doc, historical Excel, and Snowflake. Residual impact on B310/B311 pool reconciliation is small (< 1% of total). Needs upstream clarification before corrective action.

Reference formula doc subtracts B311.T56_4_2 from B311's pool. Meter does not exist in Snowflake (B311.T56-4-7 exists, but -4-2 doesn't). Suspected either typo in doc or STRUX-only meter. Not currently applied. Minor impact — flagged for upstream verification.

Reference formula doc specifies B317's T49-family terms with 0.5 coefficient (+0.5×B317.T49, −0.5×B317.T49_5_5, etc.). Our ontology allocates them at coefficient 1.0 because views.sql has no fractional-coefficient primitive for per-term scaling (same limitation as the snv_el_T49_pool annotation). Impact: B317 over/under-counts individual feeders by ×2; the net pool-residual is small because the ±0.5 terms offset. Resolution requires fractional-subtract primitive.

Several SNV EL sub-meters remain flat at zero for the entire reference period (2025-01 → 2026-02): B313.T26S-2-XX, B313.T26S-3-20, B360.T99-4-6, B207.T94-4-6. These are sub-feeders for sub-loads that are currently inactive or spare capacity. No data-quality issue; accepted.

Excel double-subtracts this meter: it appears as a subtracted −term on row 26 (B310 formula) AND on row 30 (B314 formula). Ontology enforces one-parent-per-meter and attached it to B314 (shorter-formula-wins). B310 therefore over-counts vs Excel by Δ(B313.KV1_VMM22_V) each month (+120.82 MWh Jan-2026, +26.73 MWh Feb-2026). Excel is immutable.

Excel lists B315.KV1_VMM21_V as both a +term on row 30 (B314) AND the sole +term on row 31 (B315). Ontology attached the meter to B315 (prefix-match convention). Result: B311 over- counts by +23.72 Jan / +15.22 Feb (via the pool share), and B314 under-counts by the same magnitude. B315 itself matches Excel exactly. Excel is immutable.
Device swap 2025-01-09 (last value 5386.99). Split A|B via rolling_sum.

Winter offline + catch-up flush 2025-12-16 → 2026-02-03 (47 days flat at 799.85 m³, then ramp up to 831 m³ by 2026-02-20). NOT an actual register swap — counter is monotonic, no reset event; the original "swap 2025-10-17" labeling was inaccurate (counter had already gone flat earlier). Patched via 3-segment slice/interpolate/slice + rolling_sum (:d.A | :d.patch | :d.B). Outside Jan/Feb 2026 reference window for facit comparison, but the patch spreads the post-Feb-3 catch-up backwards into Dec/Jan — see excel_comparison_annotations classification as meter_outage.

Campus aggregator for SNV fjärrvärme. Aggregates 11 FV1 intake meters (one per heat-exchanger building: B203, B216, B308, B310×4, B318, B330, B334, B339). Per Total SNV tab and Kontroll FV-mätare check meters, sums to AZ-side fjärrvärme total within 0.01 % of TN-side billing (4 705 / 4 953 MWh Jan/Feb 2026 vs TN 4 713 / 4 960). The FV1 meters measure primary-side district-heating delivery; the existing VP1/VS1/VP2 building meters measure secondary-side distribution after the heat exchanger. Conservation panel residual surfaces the heat-exchanger loss term.

Device swap 2025-05-07 (last value 1776.20 MWh, new counter starts at 84.57). Preceded by a 48-day flat 2025-03-19 → 2025-05-06 (counter frozen). BMS gateway gap on the B310 fjärrvärme substation: B310.FV1_VMM61/62/63/64 all silent 2025-10-01 → 2025-10-16 (16 days), with the catch-up landing as a single-day spike on 2025-10-17 (+81.7 / +69.3 / +38.8 / +24.0 MWh). Other intake meters (B216, B308, B318, B330, B334, B339) unaffected — gap is B310-substation specific. Outside Jan/Feb 2026 facit window.

Campus aggregator for SNV electricity at the B324 substation. Two AZ-side sub-meters feed in via feeds k=1.0: B324.H4_1 (TN Snäckviken non-Elpanna, building distribution side, ~4509 MWh/mo) and B324.H3 (TN AZ Elpanna 0678, electric boiler producing ÅNGA, ~3413 MWh/mo). INTAKE.flow = 7922 MWh Jan / 7507 MWh Feb 2026, matches Excel TN total within 0.01 %. B324.H3 is targeted at building B324 (not campus) so it drops out of the panel campus-master set; B324.H4_1 alone is the panel master (4509 MWh) vs Σ buildings (4206 MWh) → ~7 % residual = real H4_1→buildings losses, comparable to GTN B660.EL_INTAKE 8.7 %. B324 building total reads 0 MWh in both Excel and ontology because B324.H3 has feeds-out k=1.0 which drains its net to 0 by design (the boiler is a transparent conduit that aggregates into INTAKE rather than a leaf consumer at the building level).

B324.H4_1 (Schneider PME counter at TN intake) rolls over at the ~10 000 000 kWh ceiling 7 times in the observation window: 2025-03-04, 2025-05-06, 2025-07-01, 2025-08-22, 2025-10-12, 2025-12-09, 2026-02-16. Handled by 8-segment :d.A...:d.H split stitched via rolling_sum (canonical _classify_boundary detects each as rollover). Same pattern as the resolved snv_el_B308_T58 / B311_T79 / B325_T12 / B330_T65 / B337_T43 / B342_T67 rollover annotations.

TN-billed SNV KV intake = 23 310 / 19 893 m³ Jan/Feb 2026 across 6 AZ-side meters per Total SNV tab: B311.KV1_VMM26_V (TN 11065/73353516) and B334.KV1_VMM21 (TN 23772633) — already in ontology as building meters; B217.KV1_VM001 (TN 68844912 brunn 1), B217.KV1_VM002 (TN 68844913 brunn 2), B390.KV1_VM25_V (TN 53989390), B409.KV1_VM21 (TN 23772635, currently offline) — 4 wells/inlets NOT in the ontology because the existing 30 root building-inlet meters already sum to ~TN total within 3 % (22 673 m³ Jan / 18 951 m³ Feb). The wells appear to be implicit upstream of existing root meters (would double-count if added). The 6 TN meters are billing reconciliation, not topology — SNV KV is distributed wells, not a substation pattern.
